body:
## Summary
- Add explicit stop reasons for non-success orchestrated `fix-pr` conclusions.
- Post structured visible stop comments for orchestrated `implement`/`fix-pr` no-op or failure stops.
- Align orchestrator prompt/docs and add regression coverage for `fix-pr -> no_changes`.

## Tests
- `npm --prefix .agent run build`
- `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/handoff.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.js`

Closes #79

comments.body:
Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `implement` -> `review` (round 3/10).

implementation succeeded; dispatching review

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777754370677 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUyNjEyNDE0Mzk6aW1wbGVtZW50Ojc5OnJldmlldzo5MToz -->

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 5 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Comment on accepted workflow stops | pass | 6/6 | PR adds structured visible stop comments for orchestrated `implement`/`fix-pr` unsatisfactory conclusions, including action, conclusion, target, round, reason, and run ID in [`orchestrate-handoff.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/91/files). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Surface stateful failures | pass | 7/7 | Terminal/no-op states now become explicit stop output and target-visible comments instead of an opaque `fix-pr concluded no_changes`; reporting failures are still surfaced via warning logs. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Runtime behavior is reflected in both [`agent-orchestrator.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/91/files) and [`agent-orchestrator.md` prompt policy](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/91/files), including the no automatic re-review rule. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The change extends existing handoff decision and orchestrate-handoff CLI paths instead of adding separate workflow glue or a parallel dispatcher. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | The diff is limited to handoff policy, orchestrator stop reporting, docs/prompt alignment, and focused tests for the requested behavior. |

## Notes

- The PR matches issue #79’s accepted recovery option: stop visibly with enough context rather than silently dead-ending or automatically looping back to review.
- Focused regression coverage was added for both policy and CLI behavior, including asserting no `agent-review.yml` dispatch occurs after `fix-pr -> no_changes`.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific findings.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #91 addresses #79 by making orchestrated `fix-pr` no-op/failure conclusions stop visibly instead of silently ending the chain.
- The PR chooses the bounded visible-stop path: post structured context rather than automatically re-reviewing after `fix-pr -> no_changes`.
- It updates handoff policy, orchestrate-handoff reporting, docs/prompt guidance, and focused tests for the `fix-pr -> no_changes` regression.

## Review
- The main behavior is implemented cleanly and matches the accepted issue direction.
- One reviewer flagged a concrete test-coverage gap for the PR’s broader `implement` stop-comment path; Codex and rubrics reviews did not consider it actionable.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Missing CLI coverage for implement visible-stop path | WARNING | `commentOnUnsatisfactoryActionStop` now covers `implement`, but CLI regression coverage only exercises `fix-pr -> no_changes`. Add one `implement` unsatisfactory-conclusion case to lock the documented behavior. |
| Minor unsupported-conclusion consistency notes | INFO | One reviewer noted `unsupported` is included in runtime/technical docs but not in the new handoff test loop or prompt sentence. This is low risk because the runtime stop path still catches non-success `fix-pr` conclusions. |

## Progress
- Rubrics review already passed with no rubric-specific findings.
- Codex review reported no actionable findings and verified the full `.agent` test suite passed.
- Existing inline PR comments were empty before synthesis; I posted one focused inline comment for the `implement` CLI coverage gap: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/91#discussion_r3177202784
- The optional `unsupported` consistency notes were not posted inline because they are low-risk and not required for #79 acceptance.

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Missing CLI coverage for implement visible-stop path</summary>

**Cause:** The new guard in `.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts` posts structured stop comments for both `implement` and `fix-pr`, but the added CLI test only verifies `fix-pr -> no_changes`.

**Candidate solutions:** Add a small CLI test mirroring the existing `fix-pr` case with `SOURCE_ACTION=implement` and `SOURCE_CONCLUSION=no_changes`, asserting the stop comment includes the structured fields and no follow-up workflow dispatch occurs.

**Comments:** Claude flagged this as a warning; Codex and rubrics reviewers judged the PR shippable. Since the PR documents `implement` as part of the new visible-stop behavior, the regression test is a safe automated fix.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: Add the missing `implement` unsatisfactory-conclusion CLI regression test; the remaining notes are optional consistency cleanup.

## Final Verdict
MINOR_ISSUES

## Action Items
- [ ] Add CLI regression coverage for `SOURCE_ACTION=implement` with an unsatisfactory conclusion such as `no_changes`, asserting the structured stop comment is posted and no follow-up workflow is dispatched.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `review` -> `fix-pr` (round 4/10).

review verdict is minor_issues; dispatching fix-pr

**Sepo pushed fixes for this PR.** Branch: `agent/implement-issue-79/codex-25261241439`. Requested by @sepo-agent.

- Added CLI regression coverage for orchestrated `implement` + `no_changes` stops, including structured stop-comment fields and no `agent-review.yml` dispatch.
- Verified with `npm --prefix .agent run build`.
- Verified with `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/handoff.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.js`.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `fix-pr` -> `review` (round 5/10).

PR fixes succeeded; dispatching review

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777755052922 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUyNjE1Mjc4ODk6Zml4X3ByOjkxOnJldmlldzo5MTo1 -->

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 6 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Comment on accepted workflow stops | pass | 6/6 | The PR adds `commentOnUnsatisfactoryActionStop` and structured target comments for orchestrated `implement`/`fix-pr` stops with action, conclusion, target, round, reason, and run ID. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Surface stateful failures | pass | 7/7 | `fix-pr -> no_changes/failed/verify_failed` no longer becomes an opaque terminal state; the stop reason explicitly says no re-review was dispatched because `fix-pr` must succeed first, and CLI tests assert a visible comment is posted. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | `.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md` and `.github/prompts/agent-orchestrator.md` now document the visible stop behavior and the no automatic re-review rule. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The implementation extends existing handoff policy and `orchestrate-handoff` reporting paths rather than adding separate workflow glue or a parallel dispatcher. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | The diff is limited to handoff policy, orchestrator stop reporting, prompt/docs alignment, and focused tests. |
| coding_workflow / specific | Default follow-up orchestration to one-shot | pass | 8/8 | The prompt and policy explicitly require `fix-pr` to succeed before handing back to `review`, preventing repeated automatic review loops after `no_changes`, `failed`, or `verify_failed`. |

## Notes

- The current PR head addresses issue #79’s accepted path: stop visibly with enough context instead of silently ending or automatically looping.
- The earlier review warning about missing `implement` CLI coverage is resolved in the live diff by `orchestrated implement no_changes posts visible stop context without review handoff`.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific findings.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #91 addresses issue #79 by making orchestrated `fix-pr` no-op or failure outcomes stop visibly instead of silently ending the chain.
- The chosen behavior is a bounded stop path: post structured context with the source action, conclusion, target, round, reason, and run ID.
- The PR updates handoff policy, orchestration stop reporting, docs, prompt guidance, and regression tests.
- This also preserves the rule that `fix-pr` must succeed before automation hands back to `review`.

## Review
The current PR head is shippable. The prior actionable review finding was fixed, and the remaining reviewer notes are optional consistency nits rather than required branch changes.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| No unresolved actionable issues | INFO | Claude, Codex, and rubrics review outputs do not identify required remaining branch changes on the current head. Optional `unsupported` wording/test-loop consistency notes are low-risk and not needed for acceptance. |

## Progress
- Earlier synthesis flagged missing CLI coverage for orchestrated `implement -> no_changes`; the fix-pr pass added that regression test.
- Existing inline comment for that coverage gap is now resolved by the current diff and was not reposted.
- Rubrics review passes 100/100 on the current head.
- Codex review found no actionable findings and reported the PR as SHIP.
- Claude’s remaining notes are INFO-only consistency observations around `unsupported` wording and coverage symmetry.

## Recommended Next Step
NO_AUTOMATED_ACTION: No unresolved required branch-change work remains, and additional automation would only chase optional cleanup.

## Final Verdict
SHIP

## Action Items
- [ ] No required branch-change work remains.

## Rubrics Update

No changes were committed to [`agent/rubrics`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/tree/agent/rubrics) from PR #91.

no rubric changes

I reviewed PR #91, its linked issue #79, parent issue #83, PR comments, inline review comments, reviews, repository owner info, and existing active rubrics. The only substantive feedback was bot-authored or already covered by existing rubrics such as `comment-on-accepted-workflow-stops`, `surface-stateful-failures`, `keep-docs-in-sync`, and `orchestrator-one-shot-followup-default`.

No durable, non-duplicate trusted contributor preference warranted a rubric edit.

title: Make orchestrated fix-pr failures stop visibly
